title: Paper Size Reference
description: Complete reference for standard paper sizes — ISO A, ISO B, JIS B (Japan), and US sizes. View dimensions in mm, cm, or inches. Includes ID photo and resume photo sizes.
jisNote: JIS B series (Japan Industrial Standard) differs from ISO B series. JIS B is the standard used in Japan for office paper and printing.

howToUse.label: Select a unit
howToUse.description: Choose mm, cm, or inches. The table updates immediately.

howToUse.label: Select a series
howToUse.description: Switch between A Series, ISO B, JIS B (Japan), US Sizes, or ID Photo to find the paper format you need.

howToUse.label: Read the dimensions
howToUse.description: Check the width × height in your selected unit. The note column shows common use cases for each size.

faq.question: What is A4 size in mm and inches?
faq.answer: A4 is 210 × 297 mm (8.27 × 11.69 inches). It is the most widely used paper size for office documents, printing, and business letters worldwide, except in North America where Letter (216×279mm) is standard.

faq.question: What is the difference between JIS B and ISO B series?
faq.answer: JIS B4 is 257×364mm while ISO B4 is 250×353mm — they have different dimensions. Japan uses JIS B series for standard office and printing paper. ISO B is used in Europe and most other countries. When buying B5 paper in Japan, expect JIS B5 (182×257mm).

faq.question: What size photo is needed for a Japanese resume (履歴書)?
faq.answer: The standard resume photo size in Japan is 30×40mm. Some formats specify 33×48mm. Passport and My Number card photos require 35×45mm. Always check the specific document's requirements before having photos taken.

faq.question: How does A4 compare to US Letter?
faq.answer: A4 (210×297mm) is taller but slightly narrower than US Letter (216×279mm). When printing A4-formatted documents on Letter paper, content may be cut off at the bottom. Most modern printers and software can scale automatically.
